message:
fix(test): explicitly set TrueColor profile in style system tests

Two test cases (`override_applies_color_to_resolved_theme` and the
degradation affordance test at 11.1) were relying on
`StyleOptions::default()` for their color profile, which made them
sensitive to whatever the ambient terminal's color capabilities
happened to be. This caused flaky failures on terminals that don't
report TrueColor support.

Both tests now construct a `StyleOptions` with
`color_profile: ColorProfile::TrueColor` explicitly set, ensuring
deterministic color resolution regardless of the test runner's
terminal environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
